originalTitle: Cruise passengers tell of life on board ship stranded off Cape Verde after hantavirus outbreak
title: Big Ship's Safe Wait Near Sunny Islands
content:
Hello, Super Reporters!

Hello, my super reporter friends! It's me, your kid-friendly news pal, bringing you exciting news from Africa! Today we're talking about a big ship and how everyone is staying super safe.

A Big Ship on a Big Trip

Imagine a really, really big boat! It's called a cruise ship. This ship is like a floating hotel! It has lots of rooms, yummy food, and fun things to do like swimming pools and games. People go on these ships to visit new places and have adventures. Our story is about a ship called the MV Hondius. It's a special ship that takes people to cold places like the North Pole and South Pole, but right now it's in a warm place. The people on this ship were having a great time, exploring beautiful parts of the world. They were excited to see new things and make new friends.

Where is Cape Verde?

Our big ship is currently near some lovely islands called Cape Verde. Can you say "Cape Verde"? It's a country in Africa! Did you know? Africa is a super big continent with many, many countries and lots of different animals and amazing places! Cape Verde is just one of its beautiful spots. Cape Verde is a group of islands way out in the big, blue Atlantic Ocean. It's sunny there, and the water is beautiful. It's a place with lovely beaches and happy people. Our ship was going to visit these islands.

A Tiny Germ Stops the Fun

Sometimes, little, tiny things we cannot see can make us sick. We call these tiny things "germs" or "viruses." They are super, super small! A few people on the ship started to feel a bit unwell. The grown-ups on the ship found out it was because of a tiny germ. This germ is called "hantavirus." It's a big word for a tiny problem. Did you know? Viruses are so small that you need a special microscope to see them! They are much smaller than a speck of dust! This germ sometimes comes from tiny animals, like field mice. But don't worry, the ship is being super careful!

Staying Safe on the Ship

Because of this tiny germ, the grown-ups decided it was best for everyone to stay super safe. So, the ship had to wait in the ocean, a little bit away from the land. This means the ship couldn't go to the beautiful islands of Cape Verde just yet. It's like when you have to wait a little bit before you can go out to play if you have a sniffle. The people on the ship are being very patient. They know that staying safe is the most important thing for everyone. They are still eating yummy food and playing games. The captain and the crew (those are the helpers on the ship) are doing a fantastic job. They are making sure everyone is comfortable and happy, even though they can't get off the ship right now.

Why Wait at Sea?

You might wonder, "Why can't they just go to the island?" That's a great question! The grown-ups on the land and on the ship want to make sure the tiny germ doesn't spread to other people on the islands. It's like when you stay home from school when you have a cold so your friends don't get sick too. The ship needs to stay away for a little while until all the people who felt unwell are feeling much better. This way, when they finally go to the islands, everyone will be healthy and happy!

Life Aboard is Calm

The news tells us that the people on the ship are feeling calm. They are not scared. They know that the ship's doctors and helpers are taking good care of everyone. They are washing their hands a lot, which is a super important thing to do to stop germs from spreading! Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and water for 20 seconds can get rid of many, many germs! That's like singing "Happy Birthday" twice!

What Happens Next?

Soon, very soon, all the people on the ship will be completely well. Then, the ship will be allowed to go to a port, which is like a parking spot for ships. Everyone will be able to go home or continue their adventures, feeling healthy and strong. The people of Cape Verde are also waiting for the ship to be safe before they welcome visitors.

Let's Remember to Be Kind

This story reminds us that sometimes unexpected things happen. But when we work together and follow the rules to stay safe, everything turns out okay. It's important to listen to grown-ups and do our part, like washing our hands often. And always remember to be kind to everyone, whether they are on a big ship or right next to you! So, no worries, super reporters! Everyone on the MV Hondius is safe, and soon they will be back to their big adventures!
summary: A big ship with people got stuck near Cape Verde because a tiny sickness germ made some people unwell, but everyone is calm and safe while they wait for everyone to get better.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how grown-ups work hard to keep everyone safe from tiny germs, and why we sometimes need to be patient for important reasons to protect ourselves and others.

originalTitle: Orphaned baby hippo to be hand-reared by keepers at Kenya sanctuary
title: Orphaned baby hippo to be hand-reared by keepers at Kenya sanctuary
content: The baby hippo, which has been named Bumpy, was found clinging to its lifeless mother at a lake.

originalTitle: Police officer tells of operation to retrieve human remains from crocodile
title: Brave Helpers and River Friends: Learning About Crocodiles!
content:
Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly reporter, Zola, here from sunny Africa! I'm so excited to share some important news with you today. We're going to talk about amazing animals, brave helpers, and how we can all stay safe. It's like a big adventure story, but it's real life!

Hello from the Riverbank!

Africa has many beautiful rivers and lakes. They are super important for people, animals, and plants. Rivers are like highways for water, helping everything grow and giving homes to many creatures. Have you ever seen a big river? They can be very wide and long!

Meet Our River Neighbours: Crocodiles!

One of the most special animals that lives in our rivers is the crocodile! Crocodiles are huge, ancient reptiles. They have tough, bumpy skin and big smiles... but those smiles have lots of teeth! They love to swim and bask in the sun on the riverbanks. They are a very important part of nature, keeping our rivers healthy. Did you know? Crocodiles have been on Earth for millions of years, even before dinosaurs disappeared! They are like living dinosaurs!

Crocodiles are Wild Animals

Even though crocodiles are amazing, they are wild animals. This means they can be dangerous if we are not careful around them. They usually eat fish, birds, and other animals that live near the water. They are very good hunters, but they prefer to stay away from people. That's why it's super important to remember that rivers and lakes are their homes, not playgrounds for us if we see crocodiles there. We must always be respectful and keep a safe distance.

When Someone Goes Missing

Sometimes, a person might go missing. This means their family and friends don't know where they are. It can be a very sad and worrying time for everyone. When someone is missing, special helpers called police officers start looking for them. They work very hard to find clues and bring answers to the family. Our story today is about brave police officers who were helping to find answers for a family looking for a missing person near a river where crocodiles live.

The Brave Police Officer's Big Search

Imagine a very brave police officer. They had a really important job to do. They knew that a missing person might have been near the river. So, they started a big search. We call this a 'search operation'. It's like a very serious treasure hunt, but instead of treasure, they are looking for clues to help someone. During their careful search, they found something important near the river. It was something that could help them understand what happened to the missing person. This needed a lot of courage, especially knowing that crocodiles live in the river.

Amazing Scientists and Their Special Tests

Now, here's where the amazing scientists come in! When the police officer found something important, they took it to special scientists. These scientists have super smart brains and special machines in their laboratories. They can do something called 'DNA tests'. What is DNA? Imagine you have a secret code inside every single part of your body. This code makes you, YOU! It makes your eyes a certain colour, your hair a certain way, and it's unique to you, like a fingerprint. DNA is like that secret code. Scientists can look at this tiny, tiny code. Did you know? Scientists can find out who someone is just by looking at their DNA! It's like magic, but it's science!

What the Scientists are Doing Now

The scientists are now carefully looking at the special code, the DNA, from what the police officer found. They want to see if this code matches the code of the missing person. If it matches, it can help the family understand what happened and give them some peace. This is a very important step to bring answers to a family that is sad and worried. It shows how everyone, from brave police officers to clever scientists, works together to help people in need.

Staying Safe Near Water!

This story reminds us of something very important: always be safe around water, especially rivers and lakes in Africa. Here are some simple rules: 1. **Always Go With an Adult:** Never go near a river or lake alone. Always have a grown-up with you. 2. **Stay Away from the Edge:** Keep a safe distance from the water's edge. You never know what's in the water or how slippery the bank can be. 3. **Listen to Your Parents/Guardians:** They know best how to keep you safe. Always listen to their instructions. 4. **No Swimming in Unsafe Places:** Only swim in places that adults say are safe, like designated swimming areas or pools. Did you know? Crocodiles like to hide in murky water, so you might not even see them! That's why keeping a safe distance is so important.

Working Together to Help

This story shows us that even when sad things happen, there are always brave and smart people working to help. Police officers are brave, and scientists are clever. They work together like a big team to find answers and keep our communities safe. It's all about helping each other. So, remember to be brave, be smart, and always stay safe, especially near our beautiful rivers and lakes. Until next time, this is Zola, your kid-friendly reporter, reminding you to always look out for each other and for our amazing wild animals!
summary: Brave police officers and smart scientists are working together in Africa to find answers for a family about a missing person, reminding us to be safe around rivers where wild crocodiles live.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us about brave helpers like police and scientists, how special tests like DNA help find answers, and why we must always stay safe and careful near wild rivers and animals like crocodiles.

originalTitle: Our World Cup host 'is Fifa, not Trump or America' - Iran
title: Our World Cup host 'is Fifa, not Trump or America' - Iran
content: Iran's football chief Mehdi Taj says their World Cup host "is Fifa, not Mr Trump or America" and calls for respect towards the nation's delegation if the national team is to travel to the United States for this summer's tournament.
